items.title: PCA workflow from raw data to k-dimensional projection
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  raw["Raw X n x p"] --> center["Center columns"]
  center --> decomp["Covariance eigendecomposition or SVD of X_tilde"]
  decomp --> pick["Choose k via explained variance"]
  pick --> proj["Project Z = X_tilde V_k"]
items.caption: Center first, extract ordered orthogonal directions, select k, then project—this is the canonical PCA pipeline.

items.title: First principal component as widest spread
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  cloud["Centered point cloud in 2D"]
  cloud --> pc1["PC1: direction of maximum variance"]
  pc1 --> pc2["PC2: next max variance, orthogonal to PC1"]
items.caption: PCA rotates axes so variance decreases with component index; later components capture leftover spread.

items.title: Explained variance guides truncation
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  all["All p components"] --> keep["Keep top k"]
  keep --> ratio["Cumulative explained variance ratio"]
  ratio --> drop["Discard tail components"]
items.caption: Truncation trades reconstruction fidelity (variance retained) for lower dimensionality and simpler downstream models.
